title: Per-finding remediation tasks — drift finding -> work_task + advance finding (WF-015-NEG-015)
business_reason: A drift finding should produce an actionable, assignable task — not just a row. High/critical findings now create a tracked remediation work_task and advance the finding to remediation_task_created.

design.task: runPermissionDriftScan: for each high/critical finding, upsert a work_tasks row (task_type security_review, queue SEC-REMEDIATION, priority=risk, source_entity_id=finding id, idempotent task_code DTASK-<finding>) and advance the finding open -> remediation_task_created.
design.safety: finding_status is OMITTED from the finding upsert so a re-scan never resets a human-resolved/false-positive finding; only an 'open' finding is advanced. Tasks idempotent per finding.

param_14.question: How is a drift finding actioned?
param_14.answer: A high/critical permission-drift finding automatically creates a tracked remediation task in the security queue (assignable, prioritized by risk) and advances the finding to remediation_task_created. Tasks are idempotent per finding, and re-scans never reset a finding a human already resolved.
param_14.answer_source: permissionDriftRun remediation pass, work_tasks, permission_drift_findings lifecycle
param_14.security_implications: drift becomes actionable + trackable to closure; idempotent (no duplicate tasks); resolved findings preserved across scans.
param_14.approval_implications: remediation follows the work_tasks lifecycle (queued -> assigned -> ... -> completed).
param_14.downstream_dependencies: auto-assign to a security owner; same task pattern for suspicious-login alerts; SLA on remediation tasks.
